verses.text: The LORD to Moses: speak to Aaron — when thou lightest the lamps, the seven shall give light over against the candlestick. Aaron did so. The candlestick was of beaten gold, shaft and flowers, after the pattern the LORD shewed Moses. Guidance: The first thing Aaron does in his new role is light the lamps. Seven flames shining forward — light always faces the people. The priest's first work is to make God's presence visible.

The candlestick was of beaten gold, shaft and flowers, after the pattern the LORD shewed Moses. Guidance: The first thing Aaron does in his new role is light the lamps. Seven flames shining forward — light always faces the people. The priest's first work is to make God's presence visible.

The LORD: cleanse the Levites. Sprinkle water of purifying; shave the flesh, wash clothes. Take a young bullock with meat offering, a second for sin. Bring before the tabernacle. Israelites lay hands on the Levites; Aaron offers them as wave offering. Levites lay hands on the bullocks. Then they go in to do service. Guidance: A whole tribe consecrated through the hands of the whole nation. Israel lays hands on Levi; Levi lays hands on the bullock. Substitution layered upon substitution. Each blessing reaches the next.

They are Mine instead of all the firstborn of Israel — all firstborn are Mine, hallowed the day I smote Egypt's. I have taken Levites for the firstborn. Given as a gift to Aaron and sons, to atone for Israel — that no plague come when they come near the sanctuary. Guidance: The Levites stand between the people and the holiness that could kill them. They are God's gift to Aaron, Aaron's gift to Israel. Their nearness is everyone else's safety.

Moses, Aaron, and all the congregation did to the Levites all the LORD commanded. The Levites were purified, washed their clothes; Aaron offered them as a wave offering, atoned to cleanse them. Then they went in to do their service. Guidance: Three actors finished the job together — Moses, Aaron, the whole congregation. No corner cut. The Levite door opens only after every layer of obedience has been laid down. Then service begins.

The LORD: from twenty-five years upward, the Levites enter the service. From fifty years they cease the service and wait no more, but minister with their brethren in the tent. They keep the charge, but do not the work. Guidance: Twenty-five to start, fifty to stop. The older Levites don't disappear — they keep watch with their brothers without lifting boards. God values both fresh strength and seasoned eyes.
